title: Braya stigmatosa
taxonomy: Braya stigmatosa is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to the mountains of western North America. The genus Braya is a part of the Brassicaceae family, which includes other flowering plants like cabbages and mustards. Braya stigmatosa is closely related to other members of the genus, such as Braya linearis and Braya purpurascens. This plant is classified as a dicotyledonous perennial, indicating its seedling has two cotyledons.

intro: Braya stigmatosa, also known as Mountain avens or Braya, is a perennial plant species within the Brassicaceae family, native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, due to its expectorant properties, which help in loosening and clearing mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. The bioactive constituents of Braya stigmatosa include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Braya stigmatosa include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to alleviate coughs, colds, and congestion.
